query: History of the artificialintelligenceact.eu national implementation plans tracker: how many EU member states classified as Clear (designated both market surveillance and notifying authorities) over time in late 2025 and 2026. What is the trend/rate of member states moving to Clear status?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the artificialintelligenceact.eu tracker's "Clear" status trend:

**Main finding:** The tracker's stated count of "Clear" Member States (those with both market surveillance AND notifying authorities designated) went from **3 to 9**, but this occurred as a **single step change on 17 June 2026** — not a gradual monthly climb.

**Detailed trajectory:**
- The intro paragraph read **"three Member States"** with **"Last update: 19 May 2025"** and stayed frozen across every archived snapshot from **September 2025 through 16 June 2026**.
- On **17 June 2026**, the page was overhauled to **"9 Member States"** — confirmed in the 3 July 2026 snapshot and the current live page.

**On the "rate":** There is no reliable intra-period rate from the tracker's own headline figure, because it was published as a batch update rather than incremented. Net change was +6 states over ~13 months. The underlying country table was maintained more frequently than the headline number, and external sources (aiacto.eu's "8 of 27 ready," DLA Piper's Nov 2025 analysis) confirm designations actually trickled in gradually through late 2025 / H1 2026, with most states missing the 2 August 2025 Article 70 deadline.

**The 9 Clear states (as of 17 June 2026):** Cyprus, Denmark, Finland, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Lithuania, Malta, Slovenia. The original 3 (May 2025) were Denmark, Hungary, and Malta.

**Key caveat:** The headline number lagged the underlying editable table, so the tracker's published trend understates the true gradual pace of designations during late 2025 and early 2026.

sources.title: Overview of all AI Act National Implementation Plans
sources.snippet: Last update: 17 June 2026. As of the date this post was last updated, 9 Member States have designated both market surveillance and notifying authorities ('clear' in the table below).

sources.title: National Implementation Plans (Wayback 5 Sep 2025)
sources.snippet: three Member States have designated both notifying and market surveillance authorities ('clear'). Last update: 19 May 2025.

sources.title: National Implementation Plans (Wayback 16 Jun 2026)
sources.snippet: three Member States have designated both notifying and market surveillance authorities ('clear'). Last update: 19 May 2025.

sources.title: National Implementation Plans (Wayback 3 Jul 2026)
sources.snippet: 9 Member States have designated both market surveillance and notifying authorities ('clear'). Last update: 17 June 2026.

sources.title: AI Act: Only 8 of 27 Member States Ready
sources.snippet: Only 8 member states out of 27 met the August 2, 2025 deadline for designating national competent authorities.

sources.title: State of the Act: EU AI Act implementation in key Member States
sources.snippet: Member States and their designated authorities are implementing at different speeds despite uniform Regulation timetable.

sources.title: EU AI Act Implementation: German Cabinet approved the German AI Bill
sources.snippet: On February 11, 2026, the German Cabinet approved a draft bill to implement the EU AI Act in Germany, regulating national competent authorities.

sources.title: Article 70: Designation of National Competent Authorities
sources.snippet: Each Member State shall establish or designate at least one notifying authority and at least one market surveillance authority.
